sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key findings from the comparison:
- The player (blue block at [1,1]-[3,3]) did NOT move
- The black structure (node 5) changed hash (cc5204b4 → c6edccbb) - meaning its shape changed
- Node 6 (blue block) moved: boundary changed from [[8, 14], [8, 18], [12, 18]] to [[8, 20], [8, 24], [12, 24]] - shifted right by 6 columns
- Node 8 (black pixel inside node 6) moved: from [[10, 16]] to [[10, 22]] - also shifted right by 6 columns
So pressing RIGHT doesn't move the player - it moves objects inside the black maze! The blue block with the black dot inside moved right by 6 cells. The black maze structure's hash changed because something inside it moved.
This is interesting - the controls seem to affect objects within the maze, not the player icon in the corner. The blue block (node 6) with the black dot (node 8) moved right.
Let me understand the maze structure better. The goal might be to navigate the blue block to some target position. Let me look at the maze layout and try to understand what the goal is.
Looking at the image:
- Blue block with black dot inside the maze (node 6) - this seems to be the "agent" 
- Red path/structure (node 7) - could be a target or obstacle
- White areas (nodes 9, 10) - could be targets or open spaces
- Another blue block at bottom (node 11) with blue dot (node 12) - could be another agent or target
Let me try pressing DOWN to see what happens to the blue block.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The RIGHT action moved the blue block (node 6) right by 6 columns inside the maze.
# Let me try DOWN to see the movement pattern.
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
